## 6. Testing strategy

**Current state:** *no automated tests exist in the source tree* (verified — no automation specs, no test module, no test target). All verification to date is in-editor/manual (the `REVIEW-DOCKET.md` repeatedly says "Verify in-editor"). **The load-bearing completeness gap here is that no test target exists at all** — so standing one up is itself the first deliverable, below.

### Concrete deliverable 0 — stand up a test target/module (the prerequisite)

Before any test can run, the project needs a test harness. Concretely:
- Add a **functional/automation test module** (e.g. `TOGTests`, `Type: Editor` or a `bIsTestModule` Developer module) to `TOG.uproject` Modules and a matching `TOGTests.Build.cs` depending on `{TOG, TOGCore, GameplayAbilities, AutomationController, UnrealEd}` — mirroring the two-module pattern already in `TOG.uproject`.
- Land **one smoke spec** (`FTOGSmokeSpec` via `IMPLEMENT_SIMPLE_AUTOMATION_TEST`) that constructs a `UTOGAttributeSet` to prove the harness links against the GAS module — this validates the build/test loop end-to-end before real coverage is written.
- Wire it into CI / the editor's Session Frontend so `Automation TOG.*` runs.
This converts "no test target exists" from an open gap into a tracked first task (see §10).

### Highest-value seams once the harness exists (prefer the highest seam possible)

1. **`UExecCalc_Damage::Execute_Implementation` (numeric, pure-ish).** Assert `Physical + Fire` summation and `Damage.Poise` routing, the `FMath::Max(.,0)` floor, that the calc declares **zero captures**, that both output modifiers use `EGameplayModOp::Additive` (so two hits in one frame accumulate), and that a SetByCaller keyed on a tag **other than** `Damage.Physical`/`Damage.Fire` contributes **zero** (the hardcoded-set behavior, §3.3). Good test: "Physical=10, Fire=5, Poise=20 → IncomingDamage modifier=15, IncomingPoiseDamage modifier=20."
2. **`UTOGAttributeSet::PostGameplayEffectExecute` (most logic-dense method).** Drive a real ASC + apply an instant GE: lethal damage → `State.Dead` + `Die()` called once under multi-hit; non-lethal → exactly one `Event.Reaction.HitReact`; poise→0 → `State.Staggered` once. Best place to nail the docket's ordering/single-trigger questions empirically.
3. **`UTOGAbilitySystemComponent::AbilityInputTag*` (input fan-out).** Grant an ability with a dynamic input tag; fire Pressed/Held/Released; assert `TryActivateAbility`/`InvokeReplicatedEvent` fire with **no duplicates** (the "Issue 1 fix" regression).
4. **`FTOGGameplayEffectContext::NetSerialize` round-trip.** Pure serialization unit test — assert all custom fields (including `bIsGuardBroken` via `SetIsGuardBroken`) round-trip; no world needed. This guards the data carrier for §3.6/§3.7.
5. **`UTOGCharacterClassInfo::IsDataValid` + `GetClassDefaultInfo`.** Unit-test `FindRef`-on-miss returning a null-GE struct (guards ADR-4).

### Design-system seams to add as those systems land
- **Paired-execution branch (§3.7):** given a parry-result row, assert `InstaKill?=true` routes to the lethal GE and `=false` to the counter bundle — the single highest-value behavioral test for the keystone.
- **Poise economy (§3.8):** block applies poise damage to the *blocker* + sets `State.PoiseRegenPaused`; counter applies the boss−Poise/player+Health/player+Poise bundle; the regen MMC returns the §3.8 sample multiplier (higher at high HP than low HP).
- **AI feedback loop (§3.6c):** after a reaction ability runs on the boss, assert the expected `State.*` tag is present so a decorator-equivalent `HasMatchingGameplayTag` would re-select.
- **Dodge i-frames (§3.13):** during the `ANS_CanRoll` window `State.Invulnerable` is present and incoming damage is ignored; absent after.
- **Aim/ranged (§3.17, if scoped):** the projectile applies its `GameplayEffectSpec` exactly once on overlap and feeds `IncomingDamage` like a melee hit.
- **UI transport (§3.11):** whichever transport wins, assert the poise/finisher signals reach a listener when the underlying attribute/tag changes (pure C++ on either route, unit-testable without UMG).

### What makes a good test here
External, observable behavior over a real ASC (attribute values after a GE, tags present, events sent) — not internal call sequences. The two pure seams (ExecCalc summation, context NetSerialize) are unit-testable; the AttributeSet/ASC seams want UE **functional/automation tests** with a minimal world.

---

## 7. Performance & budgets

No explicit fps/frame-rate/pooling/LOD numbers are stated anywhere in `Source` or `docs` (verified). But several **code-verified facts are themselves budget-shaping**, and concrete budgets can be *derived* from them rather than from genre placeholders. Budgets below are tagged `[DERIVED]` (follows directly from a verified fact) or `[PROPOSED]` (a starting number to confirm by profiling).

**Observable facts (code-grounded):**
- **Tick is off by default**: `ATOGCharacterBase` sets `PrimaryActorTick.bCanEverTick = false`; `ATOGPlayerCharacter` keeps it off. Combat is event/montage-driven.
- **PlayerState replication frequency** raised to **100 Hz** for the player (`TOGPlayerState.cpp:17`); enemy ASC uses `Minimal` replication.
- **44 anim notifies** inventoried (24 `AN_` + 20 `ANS_`) as the window set (`TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:17`).
- **Two-archetype `{Player, Boss}`** class enum + single-boss BT design ⇒ the game is a **1v1 duel**.
- **`DeathImpulseStrength = 4000.f`** is a hardcoded placeholder (`TOGAttributeSet.cpp`).

**Derived budgets (follow from the facts above):**
- **Tick budget ≈ zero gameplay-actor ticks.** `[DERIVED]` With `bCanEverTick=false` on both pawns and montage/event-driven combat, the per-frame gameplay-actor tick cost is **0 by design** — the only tick consumers should be the AnimBP/CMC (curve sampling, §3.6a) and the lock-on component (§3.14, the one deliberately per-frame system). **Budget rule:** any new combat system that wants its own `Tick` must justify why it can't be a notify-window or a periodic GE — the architecture's stated invariant is "no bespoke tick code." A regression here (a system silently enabling tick) is the thing to watch.
- **PlayerState attribute replication ≈ 100 updates/s/player.** `[DERIVED]` The explicit 100 Hz `SetNetUpdateFrequency` sets the *player attribute* bandwidth ceiling; with `Minimal` enemy replication, the boss contributes tags/cues only. **Budget rule:** keep replicated attribute count low (currently 6 + 2 meta, and the 2 meta are non-replicated) so 100 Hz stays cheap — every new *replicated* attribute multiplies into that 100 Hz envelope. Collapsing Stamina into Poise (§5.6) removes one replicated pair from the envelope.
- **Montage-streaming budget keyed to the 44-notify / directional-grid set.** `[DERIVED]` The largest montage set is the directional reaction grid (Front/Back/Air/Block × strength × 9-cell, §3.6b) plus the counter/finisher paired montages (§3.7). With `FTOGComboRow` already using montage **soft-pointers** (`TObjectPtr`/soft refs, `TOGTypes.h:39`), the budget rule is **stream montages on-demand per encounter** (load the active weapon's chain + the reaction grid for the current boss), not preload all — the soft-ref pattern is already the seam for this. Target: resident montage set = (active combo chain) + (one directional reaction grid) + (the boss's counter/finisher rows), not the game's full montage library.
- **GameplayCue concurrency budget bounded by 1v1 scope.** `[DERIVED]` Cues fire at hit frames (parry flash, guard-break shake, finisher impact, combo-hit, §App.E) from a single attacker against a single victim. **Budget rule:** with only 1 boss + 1 player, simultaneous cue instances are bounded by *one exchange's* hit frames — propose a **small pooled Niagara budget (≤ ~8 concurrent cue instances)**, pooled rather than spawned/destroyed per hit, since the duel never produces crowd-scale cue bursts. `Niagara` is already a linked dep (`TOG.Build.cs`).

**Proposed (confirm by profiling):**
- **Frame rate:** `[PROPOSED]` 60 fps target, 30 fps hard floor during a finisher/cue burst — high headroom given the 1v1 scope.
- **Concurrent combatants:** `[DERIVED]` effectively **1 boss + 1 player**; the architecture is not built for crowds (two-archetype enum, single-boss BT).

`[OPEN]` The fps numbers and the exact cue-pool size are starting points pending profiling on real hardware once the scaffold compiles; the tick/replication/montage-streaming rules above are derived invariants, not guesses.

---

## 8. Risks / unknowns

1. **Not compiled / not playable.** `ONBOARDING.md:72` states the scaffold was written but NOT YET COMPILED as of 2026-05-30. Every runtime claim is from reading source. **Dominant risk.**
2. **Most design systems are unbuilt.** The reference's biggest systems — paired parry/counter/finisher execution (§3.7), the poise economy (§3.8), enemy AI (§3.9), the anim-curve locomotion layer (§3.6a), the windowed hitbox sweep (§3.3), the AC_HitReaction AI-feedback loop (§3.6c), the HUD/UI (§3.11), weapon-swap (§3.12), dodge (§3.13), lock-on as a component (§3.14), save/load (§3.15), the Skills set (§3.16), and the unverified Aim/ranged path (§3.17) — exist only on paper. The scaffold's orphan context fields, unset `State.Executable`, and undeclared `State.GuardBroken`/`State.Invulnerable` are the visible markers of this gap.
3. **Cross-actor pairing is half-built (§3.6b).** The defining aesthetic requires an attacker-side paired montage + directional reaction grid; the scaffold has only one-directional `HitReact` *event* dispatch (not the reference's `TryActivateAbilitiesByTag` model) with no attacker pairing and no caller for the directional data.
4. **Attribute-init ordering hazard (ADR-3).** A misordered `DefaultAttributes` GE silently spawns pawns dead; mitigated only by a `!UE_BUILD_SHIPPING` ensure + comment.
5. **Double-branch PostGEE assumption.** The design assumes PostGEE fires once per output modifier so one GE emitting both metas runs both branches; flagged as **needs in-editor verification** (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 2).
6. **Reaction-event ordering / single-trigger policy unsettled.** A pure-poise attack sends no reaction event; for combined hits `State.Staggered` may land *after* the reaction ability activates (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 3).
7. **`State.Executable` regressed pending a design decision (§5.1).** The original code unconditionally set `State.Executable` on poise break; a reviewer flagged it as overriding the attacker-authored `bIsFinisherEligible`, so it was dropped (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 6). Until the "poise-break-alone vs attacker-authored" decision is made, the finisher path (§3.7) cannot be correctly wired.
8. **Hardcoded damage-type set (§3.3).** The ExecCalc sums only `Physical + Fire` (despite a comment aspiring to a generic loop) and ignores the context `DamageTypeTag`; adding a damage type silently fails unless the ExecCalc is edited.
9. **Tag-taxonomy overload** (`Damage.Poise` under `Damage.*`) is a refactor landmine (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 1).
10. **GameplayCues are an unimplemented BP-side seam (App. E).** No `UGameplayCueNotify` class exists in C++ — the only references are the `ImpactCueTag` field on `FTOG_TaggedMontage` and a `K2_ExecuteGameplayCueWithParams` mention in the **header doc-comment** at `TOGDamageGameplayAbility.h:28` (documentation, not a code call). The cue path is not wired.
11. **Stamina/Poise duplication.** Two attribute tracks for what the reference says is one resource (§5.1, §5.6).
12. **No HUD/UI layer, and the transport is undecided (§3.11).** `UMG` is linked but no UI C++ exists; worse, the reference names **two** transports (Lyra `GameplayMessageSubsystem` ref:147 vs Aura `WidgetController` ref:394) without choosing — the entire feedback layer the poise/parry/finisher loop depends on is both unbuilt *and* architecturally unsettled.
13. **Lock-on component named-but-absent (§3.14).** The reference says lock-on stays a `UTOGTargetingComponent`, but no such component class exists — only an `ITargetableInterface` stub + the `State.LockedOn` tag.
14. **Aim/ranged scope unverified (§3.17).** The reference itself flags the sword-throw/card projectile as ❓ (sourced from `CLAUDE.md`, not the two-actor inspection); it may or may not ship — no scaffold trace exists.
15. **Config-driven wiring unverified.** `UTOGAssetManager`/`UTOGAbilitySystemGlobals` depend on `DefaultGame.ini` entries not in `Source`. If absent, the custom context + native tags never initialize. `[ASSUMED]` present.
16. **Health→poise-regen curve orientation + per-point values unconfirmed.** The reference flags the sampled curve (§3.8 sample points) as possibly axis-misread and to be re-sampled at build; the listed values are inferred from a directional correction, not re-measured.

---

## 9. Open questions

- `[OPEN]` **Tag taxonomy for poise damage.** Keep `Damage.Poise` under `Damage.*` or move it (e.g. `Data.PoiseDamage`)? Should health damage types loop a registered set rather than hardcoded `Physical+Fire`? (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 1)
- `[OPEN]` **Finisher-eligibility source — the dropped `State.Executable` decision.** Does a poise break *alone* open a finisher window (re-add the original unconditional `State.Executable`), or must the attack author it via `bIsFinisherEligible`/`SetIsGuardBroken()`? The original unconditional set was dropped pending exactly this decision (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 6); also, where is `State.GuardBroken` first **declared** (absent from `TOGGameplayTags.cpp`)?
- `[OPEN]` **Single reaction-trigger policy + activation route.** Where/when is the hit-react event dispatched for combined and pure-poise hits; and is the final route the wired event-activation (`SendGameplayEventToActor`) or the reference's tag-activation (`TryActivateAbilitiesByTag`)? How is direction/strength carried so the (paired) reaction picks the right montage? (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 3; §3.6b)
- `[OPEN]` **UI transport — `GameplayMessageSubsystem` vs `WidgetController`.** The reference names both (ref:147 vs ref:394) without choosing. Which transport carries `OnPoiseChanged`/`OnParryWindow`/`OnFinisherEligible`, and what are the exact payloads? (§3.11, ADR-7)
- `[OPEN]` **Aim/ranged scope.** Does the sword-throw / card projectile (`UTOGGameplayAbility_Projectile`) actually ship? The reference flags it ❓ (sourced from `CLAUDE.md`, not the two-actor inspection, ref:146); verify at build before investing. (§3.17)
- `[OPEN]` **AI-state-write mechanism (§3.6c).** What is the GAS equivalent of `AN_UpdateAiReactionKey` — a reaction-montage notify sending a gameplay event, or the reaction ability directly adding a `State.*` tag the BT decorators read? (`TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:43,134`)
- `[OPEN]` **Shared reaction data asset?** Do boss & player share one directional-reaction data asset, or one each? (reference defers this deliberately, `TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:134`)
- `[OPEN]` **PostGEE per-modifier firing** — confirm both health and poise branches run for one combined GE. (`REVIEW-DOCKET.md` item 2)
- `[OPEN]` **Poise base-regen scalar + player model.** The curve is a 0–1 multiplier; the base poise/sec scalar and the (different) player regen model are unstated. (§3.8)
- `[OPEN]` **Health→poise-regen curve orientation + per-point values** — re-sample `Curve_Arelius_Health_vs_StaminaRegenRate` at build to confirm direction + the §3.8 sample points (inferred from a directional correction, not re-measured). (§3.8)
- `[OPEN]` **`ANS_Hitbox`/`ANS_ComboWindow`/`ANS_CanRoll` notify-states** — tags/windows are named but no `UAnimNotifyState` implements the `ECC_GameTraceChannel1` sweep + per-swing ignore set, the combo window, or the dodge i-frame window; only `UAN_MontageEvent` (a `UAnimNotify`) exists. How are these produced? (§3.3, §3.13)
- `[OPEN]` **Anim-curve consumption (§3.6a)** — which AnimBP/CMC path reads the movement/turn curves, and is MotionWarping in the loop for lunges/finishers? No curve-consumer code exists.
- `[OPEN]` **`State.Invulnerable` declaration (§3.13)** — the dodge i-frame tag is undeclared; declare + apply it on the `ANS_CanRoll` window.
- `[OPEN]` **Skills moves (§3.16)** — the player skill set is confirmed (`AC_SkillSystem`→granted abilities) but the specific moves + `Input.Skill.*` tags are deferred; which moves, what costs/cooldowns?
- `[OPEN]` **GameplayCue wiring** — confirm the BP cue route (`K2_ExecuteGameplayCueWithParams` from the ability vs the GE cue-array) and the `+GameplayCueNotifyPaths` scan path. (no `UGameplayCueNotify` in C++)
- `[OPEN]` **Lock-on component (§3.14)** — `UTOGTargetingComponent` is named in the design but unwritten; what is its target-scoring + camera-modifier contract?
- `[OPEN]` **Save/load layer (§3.15)** — no `USaveGame`/`ISaveInterface` implementer; what attribute + unlock snapshot persists, and which actors implement the interface?
- `[OPEN]` **Weapon-swap ability (§3.12)** — `DT_WeaponState` has no consumer; how does the equip ability grant/remove ability sets and swap `Weapon.Type.*`?
- `[OPEN]` **AI Phase 3** — `E_AreliusPhases` has 3 values but only 2 combat trees; is Phase 3 live? (§3.9)
- `[OPEN]` **Config registration** — are `AssetManagerClassName`/`AbilitySystemGlobalsClassName` set in `DefaultGame.ini`? Not verifiable from `Source`.
- `[OPEN]` **Combo/air-combo DataTable consumer** — `FTOGComboRow` has no reader; which ability drives combo-step selection, and how does the air variant (`DT_MichealAirCombo`) reuse the core? (§5.8)
- `[OPEN]` **Performance targets** — fps and cue-pool size are starting points (§7), unconfirmed until the scaffold profiles.

## 10. Next steps

1. **Compile the scaffold** and run the vertical-slice spike (`montage → ANS_Hitbox sweep → gameplay event → ExecCalc → attribute change`) end-to-end — validates the entire spine; the explicitly-stated next step (`ONBOARDING.md:74`). Unblocks everything.
2. **Build the windowed hitbox sweep + cross-actor reaction *exchange* + the anim-curve locomotion layer (§3.3, §3.6, §3.6a)** — implement the `ANS_Hitbox` `UAnimNotifyState` (the OPEN/CLOSE windowed base, contrasted with the existing point `UAnimNotify`) doing the `ECC_GameTraceChannel1` sweep with a per-swing ignore set; add the attacker-side paired montage + directional reaction data on the context; and wire the AnimBP/CMC to read movement/turn curves. This delivers the defining aesthetic and the foundation the keystone reuses.
3. **Build the paired-execution keystone + the AC_HitReaction AI-feedback loop (§3.7, §3.6c)** — first resolve the §9 `State.Executable` decision; then one ability fed `DT_*Counter`/`DT_*Finisher` with `InstaKill?` as the only branch (the orphan context fields' first real caller); plus the reaction-ability state-write that re-selects the boss, closing the combat loop.
4. **Stand up the poise economy (§3.8)** — collapse Stamina into Poise, add block-cost + counter-reward + regen GEs and the health→regen MMC (with the §3.8 sample points, re-sampled at build), declare + wire `State.GuardBroken`/`State.Executable` (calling the existing `SetIsGuardBroken`) so break→finisher closes.
5. **Port the AI (§3.9) and build the player verbs + UI (skills §3.16, dodge §3.13, weapon-swap §3.12, lock-on §3.14, HUD §3.11), verifying Aim/ranged scope (§3.17) first** — BT-task→`TryActivateAbilityByTag` boss front-end + tag/EQS decorator ports; grant the Skills abilities + `Input.Skill.*`; declare `State.Invulnerable` + dodge ability; equipment ability swapping `Weapon.Type.*`; the `UTOGTargetingComponent`; and **decide the UI transport (ADR-7) before** writing widgets. Defer save/load (§3.15) until attributes/unlocks stabilize.
6. **Stand up the automation/functional test target** (§6 deliverable 0) and write the two pure-seam unit tests first (ExecCalc summation incl. the `Additive` op, `NetSerialize` round-trip), then the PostGEE functional test — converting the docket's "verify in-editor" items into regression coverage.

## Appendix — Unreal/GAS extension

### A. Component / ownership → ability / effect / tag mapping

| Owner | Type | Holds | File |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ATOGPlayerState` | `APlayerState` | `UTOGAbilitySystemComponent` (Mixed), `UTOGAttributeSet`, replicated `Level=1` | `TOGPlayerState.cpp` |
| `ATOGEnemy` | `ATOGCharacterBase` | `UTOGAbilitySystemComponent` (Minimal), `UTOGAttributeSet` (subobjects) | `TOGEnemy.cpp` |
| `ATOGCharacterBase` | `ACharacter` | cached ASC/AttributeSet ptrs, `HitReactMontage`, `bDead`; impl `IAbilitySystemInterface` + `ICombatInterface` | `TOGCharacterBase.h` |
| `ATOGPlayerController` | `APlayerController` | `DefaultMappingContext`, `UTOGInputConfig`, lazy ASC | `TOGPlayerController.cpp` |
| `ATOGGameModeBase` | `AGameModeBase` | `UTOGCharacterClassInfo` data asset | `TOGGameModeBase.h` |

`[DESIGN]` Of the original's per-feature components, only **lock-on stays a component** (`UTOGTargetingComponent`, §3.14 — named in the design but not yet written); combo/parry/counter/finisher/dodge/hit-reaction/skills collapse into shared or per-pawn abilities + tags (`TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:33-52`). The boss's component set is a strict subset of the player's (§2), which is the evidence for the shared core.

### B. Ability hierarchy

```
UGameplayAbility
└─ UTOGGameplayAbility (Abstract)          InstancedPerActor + LocalPredicted; StartupInputTag
   └─ UTOGDamageGameplayAbility (Abstract) DamageEffectClass, DamageType (class-default tag, .h:73),
                                           Damage(FScalableFloat); MakeDamageEffectParamsFromClassDefaults
                                           (C++ BlueprintCallable, .h:48 / .cpp:11; copies DamageType →
                                           Params.DamageTypeTag at .cpp:39) + ApplyDamageToTarget
```
Concrete attack/reaction/parry/counter/finisher/combo/dodge/equip/**skill** abilities and the **`UTOGGameplayAbility_Projectile`** (aim/ranged, §3.17) are expected to be **Blueprint or C++** subclasses (none exist yet). The shared attack base (§3.2) is `UTOGDamageGameplayAbility`; skills and the projectile ability subclass the same base and reuse the spine. Note the field-name distinction: the ability-default is **`DamageType`**; the params/context field it feeds is **`DamageTypeTag`** (§5.1 glossary).

### C. Attribute set

`UTOGAttributeSet` — replicated: `Health, MaxHealth, Stamina, MaxStamina, Poise, MaxPoise` (each `ReplicatedUsing=OnRep_*`, `REPNOTIFY_Always`); meta (non-replicated): `IncomingDamage, IncomingPoiseDamage`. Clamps in `PreAttributeChange`/`PreAttributeBaseChange`. **`Poise` is commented "drives stagger / finisher eligibility"** (`TOGAttributeSet.h:46`) — the scaffolded hook for the §3.8 economy. **`Stamina`/`MaxStamina` are slated to collapse into `Poise`** per the reference (§5.6).

### D. ExecCalc / effect-context catalog

- `UExecCalc_Damage` — constructor registers **zero `RelevantAttributesToCapture`** (C1 pass-through, `ExecCalc_Damage.cpp:28-33`); sums **`Damage.Physical + Damage.Fire`** (hardcoded set, ignores context `DamageTypeTag`, lines 52-62; a comment at :41-46 aspires to a generic loop) → `IncomingDamage` via an **`EGameplayModOp::Additive`** output modifier (`:68-72`); routes `Damage.Poise` → `IncomingPoiseDamage`, also **Additive** (`:78-86`). (`ExecCalc_Damage.cpp`)
- `FTOGGameplayEffectContext` — custom fields `bIsParried, bIsGuardBroken, bIsFinisherEligible, HitstopMagnitude, DamageTypeTag`; hand-rolled `NetSerialize`; **getters + setters for every field** (e.g. `IsGuardBroken()`/`SetIsGuardBroken()` at `.h:57,63`, mirrored by library wrappers at `TOGAbilitySystemLibrary.cpp:195,241`). Allocated globally via `UTOGAbilitySystemGlobals`. **These fields are the data carrier for the §3.6 exchange and §3.7 keystone; today only `HitstopMagnitude`/`DamageTypeTag` get a caller — `bIsParried`/`bIsFinisherEligible` are hardcoded `false` (`lib.cpp:133-134`), and `bIsGuardBroken` has a working `SetIsGuardBroken()` setter but no caller (setter exists, no caller)** (and note `State.GuardBroken` the *tag* is undeclared, §5.2 — a separate gap). (`TOGAbilityTypes.h/.cpp`)

### E. Notify / event / cue catalog

| Notify / event | Sends / does | Consumer | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `UAN_MontageEvent` (**`: public UAnimNotify`**, `AN_MontageEvent.h:20`) | builds `FGameplayEventData`, `SendGameplayEventToActor(EventTag)` at a single point (`.cpp:20-27`) | ability's `WaitGameplayEvent(MontageTag)` | implemented |
| *(spine, not a notify)* | `Event.Reaction.HitReact` **event** to victim (`TOGAttributeSet.cpp:104`) | BP reaction ability (one-directional, event-activation today; §3.6b target is paired) | implemented |
| `Event.Montage.ANS_Hitbox` | `[DESIGN]` open/close sweep window (needs a `UAnimNotifyState`) → `AC_Hitbox` trace on `ECC_GameTraceChannel1`, per-swing ignore → `SendGameplayEventToActor` | — | **tag only; no `UAnimNotifyState`** |
| `Event.Montage.ANS_ComboWindow` | `[DESIGN]` combo input window | — | **tag only; no `UAnimNotifyState`** |
| `ANS_CanRoll` / `State.Invulnerable` | `[DESIGN]` dodge i-frame window (§3.13) | dodge ability | **tag undeclared; no notify-state** |
| anim CURVES (movement/turn) | `[DESIGN]` drive CMC locomotion + turn-in-place + lunges while montage plays (§3.6a) | AnimBP / CMC / MotionWarping | **no curve-consumer code; CMC by default** |
| AC_HitReaction AI-state write | `[DESIGN]` `AN_UpdateAiReactionKey`-equivalent → tag on boss ASC → BT re-selects (§3.6c) | BT decorators (`HasMatchingGameplayTag`) | **unbuilt** |
| **GameplayCue** (`ImpactCueTag` on `FTOG_TaggedMontage`) | intended impact VFX/SFX (Niagara is linked, §3.1) | — | **unimplemented BP-side seam — no `UGameplayCueNotify` class in C++; only a header doc-comment (`TOGDamageGameplayAbility.h:28`) names `K2_ExecuteGameplayCueWithParams`** |

`[DESIGN]` The reference enumerates the full windowed-notify set to wire (`ANS_Hitbox`, `ANS_CanInterrupt`, `ANS_CanRoll`, `ANS_ComboWindow`, `AN_CanBlock`, `AN_GuardBreakStaminaRegen`, `AN_Hitstop`, `AN_UpdateAiReactionKey`, …) **plus the parallel anim-curve layer driving movement/turn** (`TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:17` — both branches of every montage, §3.6a). The crux for the build is that windowed `ANS_*` notifies must derive `UAnimNotifyState`, whereas the only notify in the scaffold derives `UAnimNotify` (§3.3).

### F. Input-tag → ability binding

`UTOGInputConfig` (`FTOGInputAction[]` = InputAction↔InputTag) → `UTOGInputComponent::BindAbilityActions` (`Started→Pressed, Completed→Released, Triggered→Held`) → `ATOGPlayerController::AbilityInputTag*` → `UTOGAbilitySystemComponent::AbilityInputTag*` (match `GetDynamicSpecSourceTags().HasTagExact`). Abilities self-register via `StartupInputTag` stamped at `GrantStartupAbilities`. This is the **player trigger front-end** (§3.2) and also the activation path for player-only **Skills** (§3.16, `Input.Skill.*`) and the **Aim/ranged** ability (§3.17); the **boss trigger front-end** is a BT task → `TryActivateAbilityByTag` (§3.9, unbuilt).

### G. Startup / globals

`UTOGAssetManager::StartInitialLoading` → `FTOGGameplayTags::InitializeNativeGameplayTags()` + `UAbilitySystemGlobals::Get().InitGlobalData()`; `UTOGAbilitySystemGlobals::AllocGameplayEffectContext` → `FTOGGameplayEffectContext`. Both registered via `DefaultGame.ini` per header comments (`[ASSUMED]` ini present).

### H. UI catalog — both candidate transports  `[DESIGN] [OPEN]`

The reference names **two** UI transports without reconciling them; both consume the same upstream ASC delegates (§3.11). Neither is built.

**Transport A — Lyra `GameplayMessageSubsystem` broadcast (`TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:147`):**

| Element | Type (designed) | Role | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| ASC attr-change delegates → message publisher | C++ | publish typed messages (`Health`/`Poise`/`ParryWindow`/`FinisherEligible`) onto a `GameplayMessageSubsystem` channel | **no C++ exists** |
| `WBP_*` widgets | UMG (BP) | *listen* on the channel; no controller object | **BP, unbuilt** |

**Transport B — Aura `WidgetController` broadcast (`TOG-GAS-Architecture.md:394`):**

| Element | Type (designed) | Subscribes to / broadcasts | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `UTOGOverlayWidgetController` | C++ `UObject` | ASC attr-change delegates → re-broadcasts `OnHealthChanged`, `OnPoiseChanged`, `OnParryWindow`, `OnFinisherEligible` | **no C++ exists** |
| `ATOGHUD` | `AHUD` | `InitOverlay` wires controller ↔ `WBP_Overlay` | **no C++ exists** |
| `WBP_Overlay` + sub-widgets | UMG (BP) | bind the controller's `BlueprintAssignable` events (health/poise bars, lock-on reticle, parry/guard-break feedback, combo counter) | **BP, unbuilt** |

`[DESIGN] [OPEN]` Pick one transport before writing widgets (ADR-7, §9). `UMG` is a public module dep (`TOG.Build.cs`) but no UI C++ of either flavor is present in `Source`.
